title: Andhra Royyala Iguru (Prawn Dry Masala)

subs.name: 500 g squid rings; add 3 minutes extra stir-frying
500 g boneless chicken; stir-fry 12 minutes total until cooked through

steps.text: Toss prawns with turmeric, half the chilli powder and a pinch of salt. Marinate for 10 minutes.
Heat oil in a kadai over high flame. Add dried red chillies and curry leaves; sizzle for 20 seconds. Add the onion and cook, stirring frequently, until it turns a deep golden brown, about 10 minutes.
Add ginger-garlic paste and fry until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add tomatoes with a pinch of salt and cook until they collapse and the oil begins to separate, about 5 minutes.
Reduce heat to medium. Add the remaining chilli powder, coriander powder and garam masala; stir briskly for 30 seconds so the spices toast without burning.

Add the marinated prawns and toss well to coat in the masala. Cook on high heat, stirring every minute, until the prawns are pink and curled and any released liquid has evaporated — about 6–7 minutes. The masala should cling to each prawn.
Squeeze lemon juice over the top, scatter coriander leaves and serve hot as a dry side dish with rice and dal, or as a starter with lime wedges.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Cook seafood until safely done for the species and preparation; keep it chilled before cooking.

substitution_notes: prawn → 500 g squid rings; add 3 minutes extra stir-frying; prawn → 500 g boneless chicken; stir-fry 12 minutes total until cooked through
